Ad Text:
About Us

Adaptiv is a true Kiwi success story! Recognised for our commitment to premium client delivery, we provide world-class integration, AI consulting and development service, along with data and analytics. We have a wealth of development and integration experience in moving clients from around the world onto cloud platforms.




Our integration work focuses on the exciting challenge of designing innovative API-led and event driven solutions. Leveraging the latest technologies, such as Databricks, MuleSoft, Boomi, Azure, and Solace, we develop services that unlock access to existing data within legacy business systems in new and ingenious ways.




These solutions enable organizations to drive major process efficiencies, engineer new operational workflows, and extract deeper insights from their data. Ultimately, this empowers businesses to operate more effectively and engage with their customers in modern, cutting-edge ways. Our services also include cloud consultancy, round-the clock middleware support, integration development, application lifecycle management, load and performance testing and customised integration training.




Based in our stylish Southbank offices in Melbourne, our team enjoys a collaborative, people-first culture where curiosity, initiative, and growth are valued. We combine serious technical capability with a down-to-earth, can-do approach and we’re looking for someone who shares that mindset.





About the Role

We have an exciting opportunity for a Senior Data Engineer / Consultant to join our growing Data Engineering practice here at Adaptiv Integration to drive the delivery of data engineering engagements on behalf of our clients. In this role, you will ensure high-quality technical outcomes, foster strong client collaboration, and provide effective support to delivery teams.




This position combines advanced data engineering expertise with consulting leadership. You will own engineering delivery across assigned engagements, contribute to solution design within architectural guidelines, and mentor junior and intermediate team members to maintain consistent quality and standards.




This role will be responsible for the delivery of cutting-edge Databricks technology, driving innovative data engineering solutions for clients. You will leverage your expertise to design, implement, and optimise Databricks-based pipelines and solutions, ensuring high-quality technical outcomes and efficient, scalable data architectures.




The role is highly hands-on and delivery-focused, requiring autonomous execution and a strong emphasis on billable contribution.




What You’ll Be Doing

Build, maintain, and optimise data pipelines across lakes, lakehouses, and warehouses.
Write, test, and maintain high-quality code; develop transformations for reporting, analytics, and AI/ML.
Apply data modelling principles; troubleshoot and resolve issues efficiently.
Work with Databricks, Delta Live Tables, Lakeflow, Git, and CI/CD pipelines.
Implement automated testing, data validation, and performance optimisation.
Lead unit and integration testing; ensure solutions meet quality and documentation standards.
Support knowledge sharing and improvement of internal frameworks and templates.
Act as primary engineering contact, leading technical discussions and translating business needs into technical tasks.
Communicate progress, risks, and blockers; contribute to estimation, scoping, and planning.
Mentor and coach junior/intermediate team members; conduct peer reviews.
Ensure accountability for code quality, documentation, and adherence to best practices.
Collaborate with architects on technical solutions and delivery planning.
Participate in learning sessions and knowledge sharing; adopt modern data concepts (AI/ML, data visualisation, cloud-native).
Contribute to process, template, and documentation improvements; pursue relevant training and certification.




What You’ll Bring

6+ years of professional experience in data engineering or a related field.

At least 4 years of hands-on experience developing data pipelines and transformations using Databricks, including Delta Live Tables, notebooks, and workflows.

Familiarity with Microsoft Fabric concepts and experience contributing to projects that incorporate Fabric components.

Proficiency in Python and SQL for data transformation and analysis.
Understanding of data modelling including Star and Snowflake schema design for analytics workloads.
Knowledge of Medallion architecture principles and well-architected, scalable data solution design.

Experience applying DevOps practices such as version control, CI/CD, and environment automation.

Experience with testing and data quality frameworks to ensure reliable, accurate data delivery.

Experience with visualisation and data consumption concepts, including Power BI or similar tools.


Relevant industry certifications (Databricks Professional and Databricks Associate) or a demonstrated commitment to ongoing professional development.

 

If you are looking for your next role with an organisation where you can elevate your career, this could be the perfect opportunity for you.

Who are we?
Landpower and the CLAAS Harvest Centre network is the largest privately-owned farm machinery distribution network in Australasia. We sell and service machinery to farmers and their contractors from our locations throughout New Zealand and Australia.

Our Christchurch and Melbourne offices co-ordinate wholesale activities and provide support functions. As a family-owned business we recognise the importance and value of the long-term relationships that we have grown over the last 45 years with our international partners.

What’s the job?
This is a varied and commercially focused role supporting our Product Management, Sales and Pricing teams across Australia and New Zealand. Reporting to the Group Product Controller, you will provide analytical support across sales order planning, pricing, CRM reporting, market analysis and sales programs.
You will work closely with Product Managers, Regional Sales Managers, Pricing and Costing, Logistics, Finance and Technology teams to ensure high‑quality insights inform business decisions.

Key deliverables of the role will include:

Supporting the monthly Sales Order Planning (SOP) process, including supply and demand modelling by product, region and supplier
Preparing sales, stock, pipeline and lost‑sales reporting to support ordering and allocation decisions
Maintains market analysis for Australia and New Zealand
Supporting CRM analytics, KPI dashboards and data integrity across the sales organisation
Assisting with the development, execution and monitoring of sales programs
Supporting retail machine pricing activities, including pricing updates, analysis and scenario modelling
Preparing reporting for suppliers and external partners
Actively contributing to Group objectives and supporting colleagues as required

Who are you?

You have 2+ years’ experience in a commercial, product, sales, pricing or business analyst role
Experience in pricing, sales planning, inventory or market analysis is highly regarded
Experience within the agriculture industry is highly desirable
You hold a Bachelor’s degree in business, agriculture, supply chain or a related discipline
You have advanced Excel skills, with Power BI experience highly regarded
You are comfortable working with CRM and ERP systems, data models and dashboards
You are highly analytical, detail‑focused and confident managing competing priorities
You communicate clearly and can present insights to a broad range of stakeholders
You enjoy working collaboratively and supporting continuous improvement
  
To be successful in the role you must be eligible to live and work in Australia.

We are looking for people who share our values!
In It Together, Front Up, and Keep Growing form the backbone of what is a dynamic business, providing a challenging and stimulating environment. Family is key to us – We’re a family-owned business, we employ people with families to care for, and our purpose is to provide resources and support to families in the farming community to enable their success as they feed our world.

We are looking for people who share our values if this sounds like you - apply today!

This standalone role plays a pivotal part in shaping corporate direction, driving organisational alignment, and ensuring disciplined delivery of strategic and operational plans.

Sitting at the centre of enterprise decision‑making, the position works closely with senior leaders across the business to coordinate strategic priorities, oversee planning cycles, and maintain executional focus during a critical stage in the company’s strategic journey.

Key Responsibilities

Lead and coordinate the organisation’s annual strategy and business planning processes.
Oversee governance frameworks to ensure progress, accountability, and transparency in delivery.
Act as a trusted advisor to senior executives on complex, cross‑functional decisions and initiatives.
Provide analytical insight and structure to new service and innovation opportunities.
Identify interdependencies and risks across the strategic program, facilitating alignment and timely action.
About You 

The successful candidate will bring a background in management consulting, corporate strategy, or strategic planning within a complex, multi‑site environment. You will demonstrate strong analytical and problem‑solving skills, a high level of emotional intelligence, and the ability to influence senior stakeholders without authority.

Experience within consumer services or other service‑driven sectors will be well regarded, though not essential. Most importantly, you will combine strategic rigour with a delivery‑focused mindset and a collaborative approach.

This appointment offers the chance to make a meaningful impact in an organisation navigating a pivotal chapter in its growth and transformation.

For more information please reach out to Jess O'Neill on 0431132206 | jess@itoh.com.au

Job Title: Expression of Interest - Executive Advisor - Commercial WA
Ad Text:
At GHD, we don’t just believe in the power of commitment, we live and breathe it every day.

 

GHD’s Infrastructure Advisory practice, a team of > 400 people across ANZ, South-east Asia and the Pacific, supports Government, asset managers and infrastructure investors who make informed decisions about capital deployment, operational optimisation and liability transformation. 

Our team is committed to solving complex challenges across the value chain, from capital deployment to asset management, enabling resilience, growth and sustainability. 

Together, we will help create a future for generations to come.

 

Who are we looking for?

 

We are seeking expressions of interest for Perth-based Executive Advisors familiar with areas like business case development, economic / financial modelling, project advisory, public policy, strategy and regulation as we expand our Government and Commercial Advisory team in WA. 

Based in our Perth Office, your role will be to lead key client relationships and engagements that shape Australia’s infrastructure policy and associated major projects.

Working with a high performing team, this position offers a variety of work and will see you involved in:

Using your expertise and knowledge to successfully win and deliver business cases, economic and financial modelling, and public policy and strategy work 
Project pursuits including internal teaming arrangements and contractual negotiations
Owning and delivering a variety of projects at all levels.
Establishing and maintaining effective relationships with clients and collaborating with internal GHD regions and Leadership teams 
Providing technical leadership on nominated projects as required

 

What would you bring to the team? 

 

Over 10 years’ experience delivering Business Advisory services 
Proven experience delivering Government business cases, economic and financial modelling and policy development.
You will have proven experience in working on business development and relationship building. 
Commercial acumen and critical-thinking ability 
Strong report writing, interviewing, facilitation and presentation skills.
Ability to influence others, negotiating skills.

 

GHD Advisory – clear, actionable advice that is anchored in deep technical knowledge and drives our clients’ success.

 

Interested? Express your interest now!

 

GHD is an equal opportunity employer, and we want everyone to be able to fully participate in our recruitment process. We value a diverse workforce and an inclusive culture. We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and people of all identities.

GHD embraces a range of flexible work practices to create a work environment focused on well-being, flexibility and productivity where our people can thrive. 

Be part of the dynamic culture! Our workplace thrives on a hybrid model, combining in-office collaboration and flexibility. Join us in office, three days a week to contribute, connect and excel in our vibrant environment.

#LI-MG1

Start May with more engaging sustainability projects, a pay rise and the most exciting workplace in Perth. 

Super Smart Energy is a new kind of consultancy. We are founder-led and bring together deep expertise in Energy and Sustaniability solutions, to deliver client value, and drive the Sustainability agenda. We do this by combining world leading technology and data, to guide strategy and transform business decision making by implementing sustainable solutions that deliver significant cost benefits. Our Office is located in West Perth, we have a passionate team that is focused on long-term relationships with our clients and delivering significant value to them.

Our Culture

We attract and retain top tier talent from business analytics, engineering and management consulting by providing outstanding career opportunities. We actively promote a work-life balance. We have a genuine flexible work policy, and many of our team work regularly from home when needed.

The Challenge

· Do you want to be a key part of a team that is guiding large corporates to reduce carbon emissions?

· Are you keen to use your skills to help transform businesses and create good in the world?

· Are you ready to work with a leading Managing Director (MD) to ensure that together, you can fulfil the outstanding potential of this rapidly growing and dynamic company?

If you answered YES to the above, then we have that once in a generation role for you.

The Role

As a full time Senior Carbon Analyst, you will support the business by providing in depth analysis and recommendations to clients on Carbon and ESG related issues. Your attention to detail and due diligence will set you apart from the rest. The ability to use the data to make commercial decisions is a must.

About you

We are hoping you can bring the following:

Related tertiary qualifications: Commerce, Engineering, Finance, Economics, Legal, or a related discipline.

· 8+ years of industry experience (a must)

· Carbon Accounting or Sustainability Consulting experience, ideally with a large Tier 1 Mining Company or a large consulting firm.

· Proficiency in Financial Analysis, GHG Assessments (Scope 1,2 & 3 (must have completed multiple Scope 3 assessments)), Sustainability Analysis, Data Management & Report Writing.

· Team player, you should be an adept communicator with fantastic negotiation and stakeholder engagement skills.

· Entrepreneurial strategic thinker, collaborate and embrace change.

· Wide knowledge of industry and solutions to decarbonize.

· Expertise in NGERs, NPI, TCFD, SASB, GRI and Supply Chain Emissions.

· Ability to build rapport, manage relationships and negotiate.

· Strong analytical skills and detail-oriented.

· Confident and self-aware with excellent interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal.

· Advanced user of Excel and PowerPoint.

Salary

Extremely competitive salary, based on skills and experience. Starting at $120,000 plus superannuation a year. Equity earn in available for the right candidate.

Application process

To apply for the role, please submit the following:

· A cover letter detailing why you would make a great fit for the role; and

· Your resume.

Please address your application to info@supersmart.energy.

We look forward to hearing from you soon.




Please dont submit an application if you havent completed multiple scope 3 assessments as you wont be considered for the role.

Ready to take the next step in your career? We’re seeking an experienced Business Analyst to help shape, enhance, and deliver solutions that enable efficient, high-quality services. In this pivotal role, you’ll analyse business needs, elicit and document requirements, and partner with stakeholders to design fit-for-purpose improvements across processes and corporate systems. You’ll also support system configuration and integration, contribute to testing and implementation, and drive continuous improvement to ensure solutions remain aligned to organisational objectives. If you thrive in a varied, collaborative role and enjoy turning complex needs into practical outcomes, we’d love to hear from you.

Working with us:

At The Hills Shire Council, we live our values of Honesty, Integrity, Loyalty, Leadership and Safety through the work that we perform for the Community. We are committed to an inclusive, diverse and supportive workplace culture where our people and customers feel respected and valued. Council also prides itself on being a Child Safe Organisation that is committed to promoting the health, safety, welfare and wellbeing of children and young people in our community.

What this role looks like:
Proactively engage with stakeholders across the business to capture requirements, shape project objectives, lead process mapping activities, and champion the adoption of the One Council solution.
Build and maintain strong stakeholder relationships, managing expectations, resolving issues and ensuring transparent communication throughout the delivery lifecycle.
Development, maintenance, and optimisation of Business Process Automation (BPA) and Reporting and Analytics solutions, including issue resolution, performance enhancement and system improvements
Perform gap analysis between current and desired future state processes, identifying impacts, risks and dependencies, and recommending practical, value focused solutions.
Translate business requirements into clear, structured artefacts that can be understood by technical teams, acting as the key interface between the business and corporate system.
Drive the innovation, introduction, integration, and configuration of system solutions that align with organisational objectives and evolving business needs
Provide expert process advice and guidance, prepare and execute test scripts, coordinate and undertake User Acceptance Testing (UAT) with subject matter experts, and collaborate with SMEs to identify and resolve system and process issues.
What are we seeking from our Business Analyst:
5+ years’ experience in a similar role.
Bachelor Degree in Business, IT, Finance or related field
Demonstrated experience in complex projects and collaboration among stakeholders, with solid negotiation and conflict resolution skills.
Strong understanding of business processes across various departments within local government
Customer focused with excellent communication, problem-solving and report writing skills with Visio knowledge.
Knowledge of data analytics for business process interpretation and modelling of user story maps, case diagrams and process flows using Visio or equivalent tools to clearly document and communicate requirements
Benefits of working with us:

A salary of $153,769 per annum (including superannuation and car allowance), paid weekly. Opportunities for training to keep your skills up to date. An opportunity for bonus payments and to participate in well-being initiatives and benefits available to staff through our EBA and other programs.

Next steps:

Please review the Position Description and apply via Council’s Careers Page, Careers at Council by 5pm on 15 May 2026. As applications will be processed upon receipt, early submission is encouraged.

People with disabilities, who meet the selection criteria, are encouraged to apply. If you require any reasonable adjustments to be made or if you need any other assistance with submitting your application, please feel free to contact the HR team at hr@thehills.nsw.gov.au

Please note that recruitment and offers of employment is subject to pre-employment checks including: Psychometric Testing, Reference Checks, Identity Check, Medical checks (including Drug and Alcohol Screening), Working With Children (where applicable), and Criminal History. We assess applications on merit and suitability related to the inherent requirements of the position. Only people with the right to work in Australia may apply for this position.

·         Lead the research agenda for a university with globally recognised research strengths and a bold new Strategic Plan

·         Shape a research culture and support environment that is ambitious, inclusive and built for the future

·         Drive transformational change in how UNE enables its research community to thrive

The University of New England is setting out to do things differently. This role is no exception. Regionally rooted but globally networked, UNE is home to research that matters — knowledge that connects regional Australia to national and global opportunity, and that supports individuals and communities to thrive. With a new Strategic Plan setting an ambitious direction, this is an exciting moment to join us.

The Role

As Director of Research, you will shape and sustain the strategic and operational effectiveness of UNE's research support environment. Reporting to the Deputy Vice-Chancellor Research (DVC-R), you will provide authoritative counsel on research strategy, policy, governance and compliance, while leading a directorate that is collaborative, responsive and continuously improving.

This is a role that exists to remove barriers, build confidence and create the conditions in which UNE's researchers can do their best work — from early-career academics finding their feet, to established research leaders pursuing ambitious programs.

It is also a role for someone who can lead through change. UNE is in the midst of an operating model transition, and the Director of Research will be expected to actively contribute to new ways of working — embracing simplification, AI-enabled workflows and capability uplift as genuine priorities, not afterthoughts.

What You'll Lead

·         Research strategy development and implementation in partnership with the DVC-R, including shaping institutional research priorities and managing the Research Performance Framework

·         A capable, service-focused Research Services directorate

·         The full research development lifecycle. From grant identification and application support through to reporting, acquittal and post-award management

·         A research culture that is ambitious, inclusive and well-supported, including mentoring early-career researchers and building capability across the institution

·         External partnerships with funding bodies, government agencies, industry and peer institutions, with particular focus on opportunities of regional significance

·         Institutional leadership on research governance, integrity, ethics, compliance, export controls and foreign interference obligations

·         Operational excellence. Reducing administrative burden and improving the researcher experience

·         Thoughtful integration of AI and digital tools to improve research support outcomes

What You'll Bring

·         Significant experience in research leadership, development and administration at a senior level, with a track record of building institutional research capacity and culture

·         A doctoral qualification (preferred) or equivalent senior experience in research management and strategy

·         Deep understanding of the Australian research funding landscape, regulatory environment and compliance obligations

·         Demonstrated ability to lead diverse teams, foster collaboration across academic and professional boundaries, and build productive external partnerships

·         Strategic and entrepreneurial thinking in research support — identifying opportunities, managing risk and driving continuous improvement

·         An appreciation for data-informed research management, including familiarity with research performance frameworks or researcher productivity systems

·         Highly developed communication, relationship-building and influencing skills, with the ability to operate credibly across all levels of the institution and with external stakeholders

·         A genuine capacity to contribute to organisational change, including the adoption of AI-enabled tools and evolving governance frameworks

If you're energised by the prospect of building something genuinely better, in an institution with the ambition and appetite to match, we'd love to hear from you.

Why UNE?

UNE has always punched above its weight, in research quality, in regional impact, and in its willingness to do things differently. This is a rare opportunity to help write the next chapter: a research environment that is ambitious, modern and genuinely enabling — for a leader who acts with courage, builds relationships that last, and delivers results that matter.

About the University of New England

The University of New England (UNE) is a regionally based, globally networked university renowned for the quality of its student experience and the excellence of its research specialisations. It stands as a beacon of innovation, with a legacy stretching back to the 1950s when it became Australia’s first provider of distance learning. Today, UNE continues to redefine remote learning through cutting-edge programs and a deeply student-centred approach.

UNE’s education model combines unrivalled experience with modern, flexible delivery, ideal for students juggling life, work, or remote locations. With a library of over 200 courses, robust support systems, and a recognised track record for quality, UNE offers more than access to education, it offers a future-focused learning journey designed for success in a rapidly evolving world.

UNE emphasises its people, staff and students' growth and development. We aspire to be a leader in innovation and change and a centre for the Armidale and the North-West communities.

Equity, diversity and inclusion are central to the core values of UNE, and we are committed to engaging, supporting and growing a richly diverse workforce. From the Oorala Aboriginal Centre at the heart of our campus, to the inclusive organisations such as the UNE Ally Network, the UNE Women’s Society, and the many international cultural groups - all will feel valued and respected at UNE.

Commitment to Inclusion and Diversity

UNE is committed to building a diverse workforce and strongly encourage applications from Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander Australians, people with disability, people from diverse cultural and linguistic backgrounds, mature age workers and LGBTQIA+ people.

About Armidale

Armidale is an attractive and vibrant city that combines all the features of a bustling regional town centre with the tranquility of rural living. It boasts gourmet restaurants and cafes, boutique shopping, quality schools and sporting facilities. Surrounded by mountain gorges, waterfalls and national parks, Armidale’s natural beauty and distinct seasons make it an ideal place to live and thrive. To find out more, visit https://www.visitarmidale.com.au/experience

Additional Information

Fixed term five-year contract

Attractive salary package including up to 17% superannuation (salary packaging options are available)

Relocation assistance provided

Travel will be required

Applicants must have appropriate Australian work rights for the duration of this position (UNE will not provide sponsorship for this position)

Enquiries and additional information about the role

We have engaged the services of Towardco for this role. For a confidential discussion, please contact Terri Ward, Principal Towardco on 0447 667 439 or email terri@towardco.com

To Apply

Please submit a cover letter telling us why this role excites you and how your experience meets the key selection criteria outlined in the Position Statement, along with your current CV. Send your application to terri@towardco.com quoting reference UNEDRS10250 in the subject line.

Please note that only applications received via this email address will be considered.

Applications close Midnight 18 May 2026.

Pre-employment Checks

The successful candidate will be required to undergo a National Police Check and a Working with Children Check prior to commencement. A criminal record does not automatically exclude candidates unless a specific legal requirement applies. Please visit the UNE Privacy Statement for information on how your data is managed.

About Our Process

UNE is committed to employing individuals who meet the requirements of this role and align with our values. All applicants will be contacted and advised of an outcome. The successful candidate will be required to undertake pre-employment checks prior to commencing in this role as indicated in the position statement. Please visit UNE’s Privacy Statement to understand how your information is collected, stored, accessed and used.

About the Company

An exciting opportunity for a hands-on Business Analyst to join a growing, Australian-owned organisation with a global footprint based near Sydney CBD. The business is entering a significant transformation phase across finance and technology, offering the chance to play a key role in shaping future-state processes and systems.
  
Note: Applications are being reviewed immediately, so early submission is encouraged.
  
The Role

Reporting to a highly regarded Senior Manager, you will lead a range of automation initiatives, system implementations, and process improvement programs across finance and technology. This is a highly visible role with the opportunity to drive meaningful change across the organisation.
  
About You
  

Experience working in a large, multi-entity environment is highly regarded OR a Senior Consultant/Manager from the Big 4 (Business Consulting/Transformation) looking to step into a commercial role
Proven ability to manage multiple end-to-end projects across different business areas
Comfortable working autonomously while contributing to a collaborative team environment
Strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence senior leaders across functions
Excellent communication and presentation skills
  
Benefits

5 STAR - Modern, high-quality office environment
Industry leading corporate benefits with a strong focus on health and wellbeing – including discounted travel
Genuine opportunity to lead and implement change
 To apply please click the link below!

Make your mark with Thiess  

Everyday, all around the world, Thiess supports and celebrates our people. We recognise talent and reward transparency. We champion diversity and embrace change.

Join us. Hone your skills and develop new ones. Take on big challenges and grow, through mentorship, collaboration and empowerment.  

Thiess is the place to make things happen.

What you’ll do

Reporting to the Group Manager FP&A, this role leads key elements of the monthly results process and the annual planning cycle (budget, reforecast and three‑year plan), partners closely with senior leaders, and supports effective reporting to shareholders and key stakeholders.

This is a high‑impact role suited to a commercially minded finance professional who enjoys working at pace, influencing outcomes and driving continuous improvement across systems, processes and capability.

Lead Group planning deliverables across the annual cycle, including budget, reforecast and three‑year plan.
Produce the monthly Group management results pack with clear, decision‑focused insights and commentary.
Deliver detailed performance and variance analysis across actuals, budget and outlook, highlighting risks and opportunities.
Partner with Business Units and senior leaders to challenge assumptions, drivers and financial outcomes.
Prepare and coordinate ARC and Board papers for key planning and financial reporting milestones.
Consolidate and challenge Group overheads and corporate cost plans, escalating risks and identifying improvement opportunities.
Drive continuous improvement in finance systems, reporting, controls and processes, including OneStream and ERP platforms.
Provide leadership, coaching and quality oversight to FP&A analysts as assigned, building capability and consistency across the team.

What you’ll bring

You are a confident and analytical finance professional with strong FP&A experience and the ability to turn complex financial information into clear, actionable insights.

You will bring:

Bachelor of Commerce, CPA or CA qualification
5–7 years’ experience in FP&A, including budgeting, forecasting and management reporting
Experience supporting project and/or business unit financial performance
Advanced financial modelling skills, including scenario and driver‑based planning
Strong management reporting and variance analysis capability
Advanced Excel skills (financial modelling, pivots and data analysis)
Experience with ERP and consolidation systems such as JDE and OneStream (or similar)
Strong stakeholder engagement skills and the ability to influence at senior levels
Ability to prioritise, meet tight deadlines and thrive in a dynamic environment

What we offer

Flexible, hybrid work model
Supportive, inclusive culture that values innovation and continuous improvement
Opportunity to work with an amazing team on a high impact project 
Collaborative, values-led culture with a global reach.
Access to leading-edge technology and systems.
Competitive salary and employee benefits package

About us 

Thiess works with clients in Australia, Asia and the Americas in the dynamic field of open-cut and underground mining. 

The pioneering spirit of our founders – the legendary Thiess brothers – is our legacy, and inspires our vision to harness the power of people and technology to drive sustainable change. 

At Thiess, you will have the opportunity to play a part in the global energy transition. We are growing our commodities portfolio to include metals and minerals critical to the energy transition, and developing services to enable sustainable mining. 

We’re creating a culture of curiosity, knowledge-sharing, and continuous learning – supporting our people to contribute to Thiess’ success and continued growth in sustainable mining. 

We’ll support you to hone your skills and develop new ones, take on challenges and grow, through mentorship, collaboration and empowerment. We offer our people ongoing technical training and development opportunities so they can reach their full potential. We also invest in the workforce of the future, with a range of programs for people starting their careers. 

We recognise the value of an inclusive and diverse workplace through our vision of better together. We’re focused on creating an inclusive environment to enable our people to bring their best self to work because they feel safe, included and empowered. 

Learn more http://thiess.com/makeyourmark

Should you require assistance applying for this opportunity, please contact us at hiring@thiess.com

How to Apply

This role is an opportunity to take the next step in your career. With our culture of recognition, development, and a stimulating and satisfying work environment, there is no better time to join Thiess.

Thiess follows a Pre-Employment Process for all successful applicants, which includes two employment references, a National Police Check and a medical assessment. These checks are essential to your application, and your recruiter will support you through the process.

Please click the “apply” button to apply for this role.

Applications close: Friday 8 May 2026

About Dentons

Dentons is the world's largest law firm. We're 20,000+ people globally across 80+ countries including USA; Europe; India and Asia. We are a global polycentric firm which is expanding rapidly in an increasingly globalised legal industry.

Dentons now consistently ranks as one of the five best regarded legal brands in the world according to the Acritas Global Elite Law Firm Brand Index.







About the Role

Dentons currently has an exciting opportunity for an innovative and detail-oriented Business Analyst to join our team on a 12-month contract.

As a Business Analyst, you will be responsible for enhancing the quality of our technology products by analysing business needs and opportunities. You will work closely with Organisational Change Management and Technical SMEs to design improvements, facilitate business testing, and support implementation. This role will see you working on Dentons' Case Management System project, with experience in ShareDo (Clio Operate), Appian, or other CRMs highly regarded.

With no single headquarters, Dentons offers an environment where different perspectives and innovation are encouraged. You will play a key part in ensuring that our projects are built on a solid foundation of well-documented and thoroughly analysed requirements.

Your Key Duties Will Include:

Requirements gathering sessions to capture business needs, including risk identification in partnership with project managers.

Preparing and maintaining all collateral relating to business requirements throughout the project lifecycle, including ensuring stakeholder sign-off.

Collaborating with project managers, stakeholders and business units to document and analyse business requirements for projects.

Ensuring comprehensive requirements traceability using Requirements Traceability and Testing Matrices (RTTM).

Translating business needs into clear specifications and project documentation including business cases, use cases, and process flows.

Working with project managers to develop and implement plans for successful project delivery, improvements and new solutions.

Conducting process impact assessments, feasibility studies and gap analysis to inform project planning and execution.

Participate in testing with particular emphasis on UAT, Business Pre & Post Implementation Verification.

Process mapping with focus on automation

Maintain and enhance documentation repositories and project tools (e.g., project plans, dashboards, knowledge bases).

What You'll Need to Succeed

Min 3-5 years' experience as a Business Analyst or similar role.

Experience working in the legal industry

Agile Business Analyst, BABOK or related Certification.

User Acceptance, Regression and Business Verification Testing.

Proficiency in a variety of Project tools/systems (e.g. Microsoft Excel, Smartsheet, PowerPoint, SharePoint, Teams, PowerBI, Jira/Confluence etc).

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to foster relationships across all levels of the business.

Proven analytical mindset, capable of solving complex problems and presenting actionable solutions.

Why Dentons

Dentons Australia offers a wide range of incentives aimed at embracing your authenticity, providing purpose, and strengthening your ongoing development.

We offer:



Equitable salary packaging, incentive pay and rewards
Salary continuous insurance (for permanent employees or employees on a 6 month or more maximum term contract basis)
Salary sacrificing and novated leases
5 days Bonus Leave per year after 12 months of service
Additional leave options
Equal family leave for all genders (including kinship groups)
Floating public holidays for days of cultural significance
Exclusive retailer discounts
Offices designed for collaboration on Gadigal, Naarm, Yuggera, Kaurna and Whadjuk land
Learning & Development opportunities
Generous health and wellbeing programs
Agile and flexible working for all
Social and diversity networks providing year-round events

Job Title: Sessional Lecturer – Information Technology - Canberra Campus
Ad Text:
About Wentworth Institute of Higher Education (WIN) 

Wentworth Institute of Higher Education (WIN) is a growing, TEQSA-registered higher education provider with campuses in Sydney and Canberra, offering undergraduate and postgraduate programs across business, information technology, interactive media, and accounting. As we continue to expand, we are building a strong academic team focused on high-quality teaching, student engagement, and industry-relevant learning outcomes. Students are central to everything we do, and we are committed to a supportive, structured, and academically rigorous environment that enables both students and staff to succeed. 

About the role 

Location: Canberra

Employment type: Sessional (Casual appointment) 

Appointment: Semester-based 

This role is focused on teaching and assessment delivery, working within an established subject structure with clear coordination and academic support. 

We are particularly seeking expertise in: 

Programming (e.g. Python, C#, PhP) 

Databases and Data Management (Relational, and NoSQL) 

Project Management 

Software Testing and Quality Assurance 

Industry experience and familiarity with contemporary IT practice are highly regarded, particularly where candidates can bring applied insights and current technologies into the classroom. 

Key responsibilities 

Deliver structured, engaging teaching aligned with subject learning outcomes 

Teach in accordance with approved subject outlines and assessment requirements 

Assess student work fairly and consistently using approved criteria and rubrics 

Provide timely, practical, and constructive feedback to support student progression 

Participate in moderation and academic review processes 

Maintain academic integrity in assessment design and delivery 

Engage professionally with subject coordinators and academic leadership 

Use institutional learning systems and comply with relevant academic policies 

About you 

You bring: 

A Master’s degree (or higher) in Information Technology or a closely related discipline, with a PhD or progress towards a PhD highly regarded 

Demonstrated ability to deliver structured teaching and assess student work effectively at tertiary level 

Experience in higher education teaching and/or relevant industry experience, with the ability to apply this effectively in the classroom 

The ability to integrate theory with contemporary practice in a way that is relevant to student learning 

Strong judgement in assessing student work in a fair, consistent, and transparent manner using defined criteria 

The ability to engage effectively with a diverse, international student cohort 

A clear understanding of academic standards, including assessment integrity and quality expectations 

Support and development 

At WIN, sessional staff are supported through: 

A structured teaching environment with clear subject coordination and defined expectations 

Access to academic leadership and guidance throughout the teaching period 

Orientation to learning systems, assessment requirements, and moderation processes 

Ongoing development aligned to teaching quality and scholarship of teaching and learning 

How to apply 

Please submit: 

A brief cover letter outlining your teaching areas and relevant experience, and indicating the subject areas you are able to teach 

A current CV 

Appointments are made based on teaching needs and subject availability, with teaching allocations aligned to upcoming teaching periods. 

Applications close 29 May 2026. 

Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted. 

For enquiries about the role, please contact Dr Sai Lakkaraju at sai.lakkaraju@win.edu.au

Ad Text:
About the Role

A NSW Government agency is seeking an experienced Power BI Visualisation Specialist to join a high-performing data and research team on a short-term contract through to end of June 2026, with a strong likelihood of extension into the new financial year.

This is a front-end enhancement role on a live, flagship government digital platform. The dashboards are already built - your job is to refine, polish and elevate them based on a ready-to-implement backlog of design and UX improvements.

What You'll Be Doing

- Enhancing existing Power BI dashboards based on design team feedback.
- Refining colour palettes, chart layouts, visual hierarchies and report formatting.
- Improving overall dashboard UX and presentation standards.
- Collaborating with a small team of economists, designers and communications staff.
- Supporting platform users and incorporating feedback into ongoing improvements (if extended).

What You Won't Be Doing

This is a pure front-end role, no backend data work, no calculations, no data structure changes. If you love clean, well-designed BI reporting and want to focus purely on the visual layer, this is the role for you.

What We're Looking For

- Strong hands-on experience developing and refining Power BI dashboards.
- A strong eye for design, layout and data visualisation best practice.
- Ability to work autonomously and manage a backlog of improvements.
- Good communication skills and comfort working across a multi-disciplinary team.
- High level of integrity and professional judgement - the platform integrates sensitive government and commercial data.

Contract Details

- Rate: $85/hr + super
- Location: Haymarket, Sydney CBD
- Working arrangement: Hybrid - minimum 1 day in office per week, ideally 3 (negotiable)
- Hours: 35 hours per week (7 hours/day)
- Start: ASAP
- End: 30 June 2026, with likely extension into FY27

The Opportunity

As a Manager or Senior Manager in our Management Consulting team, you will provide strategic and operational advice to organisations, supporting them to deliver measurable performance improvement in a timely and efficient manner.

You will work closely with senior client stakeholders to identify needs, shape solutions aligned to strategy and business requirements, and lead delivery teams to successful outcomes. How you will make an impact

Build strong, trusted relationships with clients by actively engaging, providing guidance and delivering practical outcomes

Manage multiple engagements concurrently, ensuring timelines, quality and stakeholder expectations are met
Lead, coach and develop engagement teams, fostering capability and high performance
Contribute to continuous improvement of quality, methodologies and best practice within the team

About you and what you'll bring

You are a seasoned consulting professional who brings clarity to complex problems and momentum to delivery. Known for your structured thinking and practical approach, you balance commercial insight with a strong focus on outcomes. You are comfortable operating in ambiguous environments, can translate strategy into actionable change, and are trusted by senior stakeholders to lead complex engagements through to measurable results. Just as importantly, you bring others with you, building capability, confidence and performance across teams.

Proven experience at Manager or Senior Manager level in a management consulting or professional services environment
Demonstrated advisory, project management and client-facing experience delivering complex engagements
Strong business and operational acumen, with the ability to analyse performance and identify improvement opportunities

About Salesforce

Salesforce is the #1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all.

Ready to level-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce.

About the Role

The Finance & Strategy (F&S) team is dedicated to financial reporting and enabling budget execution and optimization to deliver growth and efficiencies across the company. This role serves as a key Business Partner to the Go-To-Market (GTM) organisation across ANZ.

The Manager will advise on decision-making and leverage proactive analytics to accelerate the growth of our ANZ GTM organisation. They will partner with Sales Leadership, Corporate Finance, and Global Sales Strategy to provide finance-based decision support for new resourcing and investments, cost optimisation analysis, and development of key metrics. A curiosity for AI and emerging technologies — and how they can be applied to finance and business — will be highly valued in this role. They will also play a critical role in supporting GTM's annual and long-range strategic plan.
 

Responsibilities

Partner with Sales Leadership and cross-functional teams to bring awareness of business results and drive business growth

Serve as a trusted advisor to the business, helping inform and guide decision-making through insightful financial analyses

Lead annual planning and quarterly forecasting processes for the ANZ GTM organisation

Manage headcount and operating expenses, identifying areas of opportunity for better returns and highlighting areas of risk

Develop and track metrics, KPIs, and leading and lagging indicators of operational success tied to top-line performance

Provide executives with clear insights into drivers of business growth and economics, as well as helping to define key business success metrics

Deep dive on business investments to understand productivity, cost to book/serve, and ROI-based frameworks to support investment decision making

Build and maintain long-term financial models to understand new investment viability and support long-term prioritisation by role, segment, and geo

Lead financial modelling and analysis to inform trends and future planning assumptions, leveraging AI tools to enhance analytical depth and efficiency

Build executive-ready presentations including Quarterly Business Reviews, Monthly Business Reviews, and ad hoc analysis using analytics platforms (e.g. Tableau)

Provide ANZ and US HQ with updates on financial performance and ensure stakeholders have an understanding of current and forecasted expectations

Engage cross-functional, geo, and operations finance teams to drive global projects, analyses, tools, and investment frameworks

Identify opportunities to build and streamline processes, policies, and tools — including AI-powered solutions — to enable efficiency and scale

Build positive relationships with key partners across the business


Minimum Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in Finance, Economics, Engineering, Computer Science, Mathematics, or related field

Strong financial modelling experience

Problem-solving and analytical skills, great business judgment, and excellent communication skills

Experience with SQL and/or other programming or scripting languages such as R or Python

Ability to partner with key stakeholders to drive strategy and business direction

Working knowledge and understanding of sales cycles, quota setting, territory planning, and sales compensation

Curiosity about AI and emerging technologies, with an eagerness to explore how they can be applied to finance and decision-making


Preferred Qualifications

Sales Finance, Operations, or Strategy experience in a high-growth environment; Enterprise SaaS experience preferred

Hands-on experience using AI tools to drive productivity and analytical insights

Ability to influence and provide recommendations to C-level executives

Strong communicator with demonstrated ability to present to senior leadership

Experience with visualisation tools such as Tableau or PowerBI

Track record in process re-engineering and optimisation, including automation and AI-enabled workflows

Experience with Hyperion, Business Objects, or other BI tools

Ability to manage competing priorities in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment

Job Title: Chief Information and Data Governance Officer
Category:
Management (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - Federal (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
Are you a strategic thinker with deep expertise in information and data governance, a passion for digital innovation, and a commitment to public sector integrity?

We are seeking an experienced and forward‑thinking leader to drive our organisation’s information, records, and data governance agenda. This is a rare opportunity to shape enterprise‑wide frameworks, uplift capability, and influence whole‑of‑government approaches in a rapidly evolving digital environment.

The Chief Information and Data Governance Officer leads the Information and Data Governance section who are responsible for the implementation of the framework governing the management of information and data assets across the organisation. The work involves establishing and implementing frameworks, policies, processes, and responsibilities within National Archives in relation to creation, management and use of information and data assets to enable business areas to meet their regulatory, legal, risk and operational requirements.

In this leadership position, you will guide the strategic direction, design, and implementation of robust information and data governance across the organisation. You will ensure our practices meet legislative and regulatory obligations, support digital transformation, and enable responsible, ethical use of emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence.

National Archives has multiple offices across Australia, Canberra is preferred, but Sydney, Melbourne and Brisbane will be considered.

Qualification(s): Qualifications (or demonstrated experience) in information and data management or other relevant disciplines are desirable

Eligibility:  Applicants must be Australian citizens and be able to obtain and maintain a security clearance to Baseline Level.

RecruitAbility applies to this vacancy. Under the RecruitAbility scheme you will be invited to participate in further assessment activity for the vacancy if you choose to apply under the scheme; declare you have a disability; and meet the minimum requirements for the job. For more information see: RecruitAbility | Australian Public Service Commission (apsc.gov.au)

An exciting opportunity for an Operational Technology Cyber Security Business Analyst to join a public sector organisation in Brisbane. You'll lead discovery, requirements, and gap analysis activities across OT and cyber initiatives, working with diverse stakeholders to deliver secure, resilient solutions that support critical infrastructure.

An exciting opportunity is available for a highly skilled Operational Technology Cyber Security Business Analyst to join a forward‑thinking public sector organisation in Brisbane. This pivotal role is ideal for someone who enjoys delivering meaningful outcomes by shaping technology initiatives across OT and cyber security environments.

You will be central to initiatives that enhance security, efficiency, and compliance across critical infrastructure, working closely with internal stakeholders and delivery partners to support seamless OT and cyber security implementations. The organisation offers a supportive, inclusive workplace with flexible working arrangements and ongoing professional development, enabling you to grow your career while making a genuine impact.

Why join?

Play a key role in technology initiatives that strengthen OT and cyber security across essential services
Enjoy flexible working arrangements that support work-life balance
Collaborate with experienced, values-driven teams in a supportive and inclusive environment
What you'll do

As an OT Cyber Security Business Analyst, you will play a central role in the success of initiatives that underpin critical operations. You will lead structured discovery activities, engage stakeholders across the organisation, and translate business needs into clear, testable requirements aligned with industry best practice.

Key responsibilities include:

Leading discovery sessions to understand current-state processes, systems, roles, responsibilities, and constraints within OT and cyber environments
Conducting detailed gap analyses against standards such as IS18 and ISO27000, identifying risks and opportunities for improvement
Facilitating stakeholder engagement through interviews and workshops to gather and validate requirements
Developing comprehensive business cases outlining problems, benefits, costs, risks, and delivery options
Preparing accurate and complete project initiation artefacts aligned to strategic and operational objectives
Producing prioritised, traceable, and testable requirements documentation to support internal teams and third‑party vendors
Maintaining robust requirements traceability from initiation through to solution acceptance
Supporting testing, acceptance, and transition into business‑as‑usual operations
Reviewing and redesigning processes and workflows to improve security, resilience, and long‑term sustainability

Your proactive approach and attention to detail will help identify issues early, support continuous improvement, and ensure secure, future‑ready outcomes.

What you bring

You will bring strong experience as a Business Analyst within operational technology and/or cyber security environments, with a proven ability to translate complex needs into practical, value-driven solutions.

Key skills and experience include:

Demonstrated experience leading structured discovery activities in OT or cyber security settings
Proven capability performing gap analyses against IS18, ISO27000, or similar frameworks
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, including facilitating workshops across multidisciplinary teams
Experience developing well-structured business cases for executive decision-making
High attention to detail when producing project initiation and requirements documentation
Advanced capability in producing testable, traceable requirements across the project lifecycle
Experience working with vendors and maintaining alignment through traceability frameworks
Ability to provide clear artefacts such as process maps, acceptance criteria, and requirements specifications
A proactive, collaborative mindset with a focus on continuous improvement and risk mitigation
Strong process analysis and redesign skills to enhance operational efficiency, security, and resilience

The Company

Robert Half Executive Search has been exclusively retained to identify an accomplished Technology Risk & Assurance professional for an Associate Director position.

This globally renowned and respected firm is committed to continued growth in the APAC region. The firm's consultancy services span Technology Assurance, Internal Audit, Risk and Compliance Advisory, Technology, Cybersecurity, and Business Performance Improvement across multiple industries in Corporate, Financial Services and Government.

Our client promotes an agile and flexible work environment, and it is for this reason that the firm has been consistently endorsed as an Employer of Choice. Unique to this firm is the ability to be financially rewarded on a personal level commensurate with performance as well as that of the organisation as a whole.

The Role

The Associate Director supports the growth and operational success of the practice while leading a team of consultants to ensure exceptional client satisfaction.

In this role, they oversee multiple programs of work across Data & AI, Technology, Internal Audit and Cyber, partnering closely with Managing Directors and colleagues to tailor each engagement to the client's specific needs.

Role Responsibilities

Lead complex technology assurance engagements, managing senior stakeholder expectations and ensuring the delivery of high‑quality work and client outcomes.
Collaborate closely with CIOs, CFOs and other executives to help organisations maximise the return on their technology investments while minimising risk.
Apply strong technology governance frameworks to align technology initiatives with business strategy and enhancing technology infrastructure.
Provide organisations with clear insights into technology‑related risks, assessing threats, control environments and their impact on critical business processes.
Drive growth of the Technology Assurance portfolio by strengthening and expanding client relationships while also supporting the firm's visibility through go‑to‑market initiatives and broader practice positioning.
Mentor and guide technology assurance professionals, building capability and supporting team development.

The firm believes in empowering its people, providing an autonomous working environment where employees enjoy the freedom to generate exciting and challenging pieces of work and focus on areas of particular interest.

Your Profile

An articulate and credible consulting professional, you will be a highly accomplished Senior Manager or Associate Director within a Big 4 firm or boutique Technology Consulting practice. You may also be currently working in an industry-based technology assurance role and looking to return to a consulting environment. Previous consulting experience is essential.


You will bring a strong foundation in Assurance and Advisory, with the ability to apply your expertise across multiple technology and risk disciplines with experience across at least two domains within Data & AI, Technology, Internal Audit or Cyber.

Known as a thought leader, the successful individual will be a proven, trusted client advisor with an established local network in the Sydney market and will possess a deep understanding of relevant current and emerging technologies and methodologies.

A track record of balancing execution / delivery activities will set you up for an outstanding career with this growing firm. You'll also receive ongoing support to build your capabilities, including regular networking opportunities to help uncover and develop new business.

With strong stakeholder engagement skills, the ideal candidate will have a genuine interest in building long-term relationships with a diverse portfolio of clients, aiming to retain existing clients and generate new business opportunities for the firm through solving clients' problems.

In addition, you will bring:

Proven experience of leading and executing Technology Assurance engagements with a deep understanding of the challenges that organisations encounter around the use of existing and emerging technologies.
Capability to lead the end-to-end process.
Prior exposure to supervising in a team environment, coaching and developing strong teams and fostering interpersonal connections.
A willingness to drive business growth with an interest in discovering new opportunities with current and potential clients through thought leadership, proposal writing, and client interactions.

What the firm can offer to prospective Employees

Tailored progression and promotion pathways.
Exposure to diverse projects across major Australian organisations.
A collaborative culture that supports learning, growth and advancement.
Consistently recognised as a great place to work.
Flexible working arrangements that support work-life balance.
A strong national and global growth trajectory.

Benefits include an attractive compensation with bonus potential, professional membership and study support, generous leave, DEI networking groups, and access to a global mobility program

Company: Sunshine Coast Hospital and Health Service
Ad Text:
We're excited to welcome a Principal Data Analyst – Scientific Analysis, who performs applied analytical investigations to inform operational and clinical decision-making across Sunshine Coast Hospital and Health Service.

The role works with clinicians, managers and executives to clarify problems, translate them into structured analytical approaches, and deliver evidence-based findings that support action.

Sunshine Coast Health is a values-based organisation. We are committed to the maintenance of a culture in which we treat our colleagues with respect as we do our patients, consumers and the community. We respect, protect and promote human rights in our decision-making and actions.

Key Responsibilities include:

Engage with clinicians, managers and stakeholders to clarify operational and clinical problems and translate them into structured analytical approaches and work plans, for example understanding drivers of overcrowding, delays to discharge or variation in care pathways.
Plan and lead defined analytical work packages and small projects, managing scope, timelines and deliverables within a busy operational environment.
Undertake complex data extraction, preparation and analysis using SQL and analytical programming languages such as R or Python.

Remuneration value up to $171,568 p.a., comprising salary between $140,115 - $150,371 p.a., employer contribution to superannuation (12.75%) and annual leave loading (17.5%) (iAO7). Applications will remain current for 12 months. Please note: only applications from candidates will be accepted (applications that may result in an agency fee will not be considered).

Why work for us?

We offer rewarding career opportunities across a wide range of clinical and non-clinical areas across our health service and pride ourselves on providing a work environment that is safe, satisfying, flexible, and promotes a healthy work-life balance.

As a Queensland Health employee, you will benefit from a higher than standard employer contribution to Superannuation of up to 12.75%, access to salary packaging, flexible working arrangements and competitive salary rates with annual incremental increases.

We are committed to providing a diverse and inclusive workplace for our people and our community. We encourage people of all genders, races, ages and abilities to apply for roles within our Health Service.

You can find out more about why it's so great to work at Sunshine Coast here: https://www.health.qld.gov.au/sunshinecoast/careers

A wellness program;
Generous superannuation;
Flexible work arrangements;
Career training and development; and
Salary packaging.
